summary: Comprehensive unit test coverage for all major functions (Parse-PackageJson, Parse-RequirementsTxt, Get-LicenseStatus, Invoke-LicenseCheck, Format-ComplianceReport) with good end-to-end scenarios. Workflow structure tests validate YAML, actionlint, and file references. Strengths: clear Describe/It organization, logical test grouping, both manifest types tested, all three license statuses (approved/denied/unknown) verified, error handling for missing files and unsupported formats. Gaps: (1) No test definitions for referenced fixture files (fixtures/package.json, fixtures/requirements.txt, fixtures/license-config.json) — test harness copies nonexistent files, causing immediate failure; (2) Limited edge case rigor (no malformed JSON, empty manifests, complex version specifiers like >=1.0 or !=2.0); (3) run-tests.ps1 contains brittle string matching for act output (expects "Correctly detected denied licenses" which doesn't appear in Format-ComplianceReport implementation); (4) No explicit whitespace/comments handling verification in Parse-RequirementsTxt despite parsing logic supporting it. Would catch basic regressions in core logic but fails to run due to missing fixtures and has insufficient boundary condition coverage.
